autom8y::autom8y-asana::feat/index.domain_summary: # autom8y::autom8y-asana::feat/index This knowledge domain documents a comprehensive census of 26 features across the autom8y-asana SDK, categorized into core platform infrastructure, business domain modeling, and automation systems. The domain maps architectural patterns, implementation evidence, and documentation artifacts to guide code generation and development priorities, with 23 features recommended for GENERATE and 3 for SKIP based on complexity, confidence, and user-facing impact.

section_summaries.asana-models: 15+ Pydantic v2 resource models (task, project, section, user, webhook, goal, portfolio, custom_field, tag, story, team, workspace) with custom field catalog documenting 108 fields across 5 entity types.
section_summaries.business-domain-model: 60-file domain model package encompassing Business, Unit, Contact, Offer, Process, Location, Hours, AssetEdit, Videography, DNA entities with hydration, reconciliation, seeding, and entity-lifecycle guidance.
section_summaries.cache-subsystem: Multi-tier intelligent cache system spanning 52 files with memory/Redis/S3 backends, dataframe layer optimization, mutation invalidation, staleness detection, and 6 reference documents plus troubleshooting runbook.
section_summaries.data-attachment-bridge: Cross-cutting pattern enabling backend-to-Asana reporting pipelines (ConversationAuditWorkflow, InsightsExportWorkflow) that share WorkflowAction contract, AttachmentReplacementMixin, DataServiceClient, and Lambda factory infrastructure.
section_summaries.dataframe-layer: Polars-based analytics layer with 47 files covering progressive builders, schema definitions (contact, offer, asset_edit), extractors, and views exposed via user-facing REST endpoint.
section_summaries.entity-detection: 8-file multi-tier detection system (tiers 1-4) classifying Asana tasks into business entity types, with tier specification reference, troubleshooting runbook, and diagnostic scripting.
section_summaries.event-emission: Event-driven architecture emitting structured TaskEvent and StoryEvent messages to kafka/sns/sqs backends with event registry, type validation, and integration with persistence mutations.
section_summaries.fuzzy-entity-matching: Fuzzy matching engine for entity deduplication enabling Contact and Offer consolidation through Levenshtein distance and semantic similarity scoring with reconciliation integration.
section_summaries.http-transport: Asana-specific HTTP transport layer wrapping autom8y_http with adaptive semaphore concurrency control, rate limiting, circuit breaker, and retry configurations documented in rate-limiting runbook.
section_summaries.query-engine: DataFrame query engine with 19 files implementing compiled predicates, cross-entity joins, aggregation, temporal/timeline queries, and CLI interface with 3 search guides and API reference.
section_summaries.resource-clients: 17 client files (tasks, projects, sections, users, workspaces, webhooks, goals, portfolios, tags, stories, attachments, teams, custom_fields, batch) sharing `BaseClient` pattern with dedicated API reference.
section_summaries.save-session: Unit of work pattern implemented across 20 files providing dependency ordering, cascade execution, healing, and holder concurrency with dedicated guide, lifecycle reference, and debugging runbook.
section_summaries.sdk-client-facade: Primary user-facing `AsanaClient` entry point providing lazy-initialized, thread-safe access to 17+ resource clients with dedicated SDK reference documentation and README prominence.

autom8y::autom8y-asana::feat/lambda-hand.domain_summary: # AWS Lambda Function Handlers This feature domain implements event-driven Lambda handlers for the autom8y-asana system, solving cache pre-warming, maintenance, and scheduled workflow automation. The system uses a single container image with dual-mode execution (ECS HTTP server vs. Lambda event handler) selected at runtime via the `AWS_LAMBDA_RUNTIME_API` environment variable, with handler paths passed as command-line arguments for auditability.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Cache warmer's 15-minute timeout is mitigated by checkpoint-based resumption with `_should_exit_early()` buffer logic and async self-invocation; non-blocking side effects (GID push, story cache warm) fail gracefully without propagating; checkpoint staleness (1-hour default) prevents resume loops; workflow handlers validate input scope and emit metrics only on success; the single container image trades cold-start latency for operational simplicity.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: The system operates as two runtime personalities—ECS (long-lived HTTP server consuming cache) and Lambda (short-lived event handlers populating/maintaining cache or executing scheduled workflows)—with six handler types organized as three cache-maintenance handlers (`cache_warmer`, `cache_invalidate`, `checkpoint`) and three workflow handlers (`insights_export`, `conversation_audit`, factory-created handlers), all coordinated through the `EntityScope` dataclass that flows invocation parameters from Lambda event boundary into workflow execution.
section_summaries.implementation-map: The implementation provides `WarmResponse` and `InvalidateResponse` dataclasses for structured returns, `CheckpointRecord` and `CheckpointManager` for S3-backed resume-on-retry with staleness detection, `WorkflowHandlerConfig` (frozen dataclass) and `create_workflow_handler()` factory for parameterized workflow instantiation, and shared `emit_metric()` utility across all handlers, with dual-mode entry via `entrypoint.py` dispatching to `awslambdaric.main()` when `AWS_LAMBDA_RUNTIME_API` is present.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Trade-offs between S3 checkpoint atomicity and concurrent invocation collisions are not documented; optimal `cascade_warm_order()` entity priority (providers before consumers) rationale lacks justification; cost/benefit analysis of single-image dual-mode vs. separate ECS/Lambda images is missing; failure modes from `_self_invoke_continuation()` async invocation (e.g., throttling, permission failures) lack documented recovery strategies.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: AWS Lambda handlers solve cold-cache startup latency and API rate-limit problems through pre-populated S3/Redis caches (via `cache_warmer`), handle 15-minute timeout overruns using S3-backed checkpoints with self-invocation, apply a DRY workflow factory pattern (`WorkflowHandlerConfig` + `create_workflow_handler()`) to eliminate boilerplate across `insights_export` and `conversation_audit`, and implement dead-man's-switch metrics via `emit_success_timestamp()` for Grafana alerting.

autom8y::autom8y-asana::release/history.domain_summary: autom8y-asana::release/history documents the deployment history and outcomes of the autom8y-asana service, a satellite receiver component for the autom8y automation platform. The domain tracks PATCH and HOTFIX releases with detailed pipeline execution metrics, failure modes, and infrastructure fixes across the test → satellite-dispatch → satellite-receiver workflow chain. log | Tracks four releases from 2026-03-03 to 2026-03-15, including a 4-round fail-forward PATCH (AUTOM8Y_ env var standardization with Terraform null field fixes), three single-day hotfixes (cascade fixes for hierarchy warmup and parent_gid extraction), and one multi-issue PATCH that resolved reusable workflow resolution, ruff linting violations, and org secrets visibility following CI migration to autom8y-workflows.

autom8y::autom8y-asana::release/platform.domain_summary: autom8y-asana is a Python-based container application (v1.0.0) deployed to ECS via GitHub Actions, using UV for dependency management and a three-stage CI/CD pipeline. The platform employs automated satellite dispatch workflows that trigger builds and deployments upon successful test completion, with OIDC-based AWS authentication and GitHub API-stored attestations. Repository | Python UV ecosystem (pyproject.toml, uv.lock) with Docker/ECS distribution, public visibility, 26 Justfile targets, no dependents. Pipeline Chain (depth 3) | Three-stage workflow: test.yml runs linting/type-checking/pytest with ≥80% coverage on push; satellite-dispatch.yml triggers on test success via GitHub App token; satellite-receiver.yml in autom8y/autom8y builds Docker image with Trivy/SBOM, deploys Lambda via Terraform, and performs rolling ECS deployment with smoke tests. Key Configuration | Reusable workflows from autom8y-workflows, org secrets for GitHub App auth, AWS IAM OIDC role for CodeArtifact, pre-commit hooks (ruff/mypy strict mode), manual dispatch escape hatch on satellite-dispatch only, attestations stored on GitHub API. Known Flakiness | ECS ServicesStable waiter exhibits ~50% transient timeouts on first attempt (resolved on retry, timing-related not code-related); Sigstore attestation 401s reduced after switching to GitHub API storage in March 2026. Release Action | Push to main automatically triggers test.yml; on CI pass, satellite-dispatch fires automatically; on dispatch, satellite-receiver builds and deploys to ECS/Lambda without version bumping or registry publishing. Env Var Convention | Settings use AUTOM8Y_ prefix for Tier 2 variables (AUTOM8Y_DATA_URL, AUTOM8Y_DATA_CACHE_TTL, AUTOM8Y_DATA_INSIGHTS_ENABLED) and ASANA_RUNTIME_ prefix for feature flags; tests must use prefixed names and call reset_settings() when patching.

autom8y::autom8y-asana::scar-tissue.domain_summary: This knowledge domain is a comprehensive catalog of 16+ production failures and deployment scars in the autom8y-asana codebase, each with commit evidence, fix locations, and defensive patterns to prevent recurrence. The catalog serves as institutional memory for architectural vulnerabilities across entity resolution, cache synchronization, cascade field validation, authentication, and ECS deployment—enabling developers to understand root causes and implement preventive safeguards.

section_summaries.agent-relevance-tagging: Scars are tagged with ADR markers (ADR-HOTFIX-entity-collision, ADR-HOTFIX-002), DEF codes (DEF-001, DEF-005, DEF-006/7/8), DEBT codes (DEBT-003, DEBT-005), and F- prefixes (F-1) to cross-reference related architectural decisions and known gaps.
section_summaries.category-coverage: Failures span entity collision (UnitHolder/Unit GID), cache deadlock (orphaned IN_PROGRESS sections), stale artifact persistence (force-rebuild S3 cleanup), cache provider isolation (warm-up visibility gaps), cascade field nulls (hierarchy warming gaps, parent_gid persistence), SWR all-sections-skipped logic, snapshot ordering (DEF-001), async context sync calls, thread-safety (DEBT-003/005), ECS liveness blocking, S2S auth (JWT vs raw Bearer tokens), and schema SDK version mismatches.
section_summaries.defensive-pattern-documentation: Recurring patterns include PRIMARY_PROJECT_GID tier-1 resolution guards, in_progress_since timeout handling, full S3 artifact purge on force-rebuild, shared CacheProvider at app.state, parent_gid parquet persistence for hierarchy reconstruction, cascade null-rate thresholds (WARN 5%, ERROR 20%), chain-traversal gap skipping, BuildResult.total_rows merged DataFrame fallback, post-commit snapshot ordering, CI ASANA_WORKSPACE_GID env var guards, SaveSession RLock for state transitions, and liveness/readiness health check separation.
section_summaries.failure-catalog: 16 confirmed production/near-production failures (SCAR-001 through SCAR-022) and 4 deployment scars are documented with commit hashes, root causes, and fix commit evidence ranging from 2026-03-18 genesis through incremental post-release additions.
section_summaries.fix-location-mapping: Each scar references specific file paths and line numbers for fixes—e.g., SCAR-001 routes through `src/autom8_asana/services/discovery.py` line 29, SCAR-005 persists `parent_gid` in `src/autom8_asana/dataframes/schemas/base.py`, SCAR-011 separates liveness/readiness in `src/autom8_asana/api/routes/health.py`—enabling pinpoint code navigation.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: SCAR-004 has no dedicated isolated-provider regression test; SCAR-008 lacks isolated regression test coverage; SCAR-013 entry is truncated in source; missing guidance on production monitoring thresholds for cascade null-rate anomaly detection; no mapped escalation procedures for concurrent failures.

autom8y::autom8y-asana::test-coverage.domain_summary: This knowledge domain documents the test coverage strategy, conventions, and gaps for autom8y-asana, a Python 3.12 automation platform using pytest with 443 test files (11,590 tests) across 392 source files with an enforced 80% coverage threshold in CI. coverage-gaps | Two packages have zero test coverage: `_defaults/` (platform SDK wiring, medium criticality) and `protocols/` (pure PEP 544 interfaces, intentional); additional gaps include `cloudwatch.py` lambda handler, `entity_context.py` service, and 10 actively skipped tests representing deferred bugs and stale test code. testing-conventions | Tests use descriptive `test_{what_is_being_tested}` naming in 2,690 class-based clusters (vs 190 standalone functions), with 2,496 async tests using `@pytest.mark.asyncio`, 93 parametrized cases, 1,170 `pytest.raises` exception assertions, 706 mock verifications, and 12 conftest files providing 143 factory functions and fixtures (MockClientBuilder, domain entities, lifecycle config). test-structure-summary | Unit tests (405 files, ~11,000 tests) mock all external I/O and run on PRs; integration tests (31 files, ~350 tests) either require live `ASANA_PAT` credentials or wire real components without external calls; heaviest coverage is in cache (56 files), automation (47 files), and dataframes (43 files); CI enforces 80% threshold excluding integration and benchmark markers on PRs, including them only on main branch pushes. knowledge-gaps | No coverage report artifact was present to validate actual per-module percentages against the 80% threshold; the specific coverage % for lightly-covered packages (`observability/`, `search/`, `patterns/`) is unknown; live API integration test results and pass rates are not documented.

autom8y::autom8y-data::architecture.domain_summary: autom8y::autom8y-data::architecture is a four-layer Python database platform for the autom8 ecosystem comprising 36 sub-packages (~220 files) with explicit layer boundaries: Layer 1 (core data access via SQLModel repositories), Layer 2 (analytics semantic layer with metric registry and query pipeline), Layer 3 (semantic analytics, optional), and Layer 4 (FastAPI REST and gRPC APIs). The architecture uses optional extras to gate layers and enforces unidirectional dependencies from higher to lower layers, with hub packages (core, analytics.core, analytics.engine) serving as aggregation points for imports.

section_summaries.data-flow: Data flows from REST/gRPC API through analytics engine (metrics, insights, post-processors) to core repositories and SQLModel entities; query execution uses `QueryPlanner`→`QueryBuilder`→`SQLGenerator`→`QueryExecutor` with join path optimization via `JoinPathOptimizer` and caching via `QueryCache`.
section_summaries.entry-points-and-api-surface: Layer 4 provides dual API surfaces: FastAPI via `create_app()` factory with ~30 CRUD/analytics routers and gRPC via `GRPCServer` with protocol-buffer-generated handlers for business, lead, appointment, and payment entities.
section_summaries.key-abstractions: Core abstractions include `Repository` (typed repository pattern over SQLModel), `MetricRegistry` (stores dimensions/metrics with composite metric and formula support), `QueryBuilder`/`QueryPlanner`/`QueryExecutor` (analytics query pipeline), `JoinGraph` (NetworkX-based join optimization), and `AnalyticsRequest` (Pydantic request translation with time/scope resolution).
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Implementation details of semantic analytics Layer 3, specific circular import patterns in joins/metrics_availability/registry modules, materialization scheduler fault tolerance, and distributed lock mechanism for scheduler are not fully documented.
section_summaries.layer-boundaries: Four explicit layers enforce unidirectional import flow (Layer 4→1) with documented dependency rules per module; only one documented cross-layer violation exists (analytics.services→api.data_service_models), and three modules require mypy circular import suppression.
section_summaries.package-structure: The project uses a single installable package `autom8_data` with 36 sub-packages organized hierarchically, including hub packages (core, analytics.core, analytics.engine) that re-export symbols and leaf packages (proto, config, models, schemas) with minimal dependencies.

autom8y::autom8y-data::conventions.domain_summary: The autom8y-data codebase implements a multi-layer error hierarchy with semantic, service, scheduler, and auth exceptions, each exposing different attributes for their context (rich dataclasses with fuzzy-matched suggestions vs. gRPC/HTTP status codes). File organization enforces layered architecture across seven top-level packages with dependency rules documented inline, while domain-specific patterns include typed registries, `__table_type__` discovery for SQLModel auto-classification, and compositional context managers for transactional safety.

section_summaries.domain-specific-idioms: The codebase uses typed registry classes (`MetricRegistry`, `InsightRegistry`, `MaterializationRegistry`) exposing `register_*()` and `get_*()` methods, `__table_type__` string attributes on SQLModel classes for auto-classification discovery, compositional `transactional()` context managers that combine `db_error_boundary()` and session management without service-layer commits, `BatchWriteResult[T]` dataclasses for partial-success batch mutations, and `rapidfuzz`-powered fuzzy suggestions for typo correction in error messages.
section_summaries.error-handling-style: The codebase maintains three distinct error hierarchies—`SemanticError` with lazy-computed fuzzy-matched suggestions, `ServiceError` with protocol-agnostic status/code attributes, and `SchedulerError`—with a `db_error_boundary()` async context manager translating raw SQLAlchemy exceptions at route boundaries and FastAPI exception handlers converting all domain exceptions to `{"error": {"code": "...", "message": "...", "request_id": "..."}}` JSON responses.
section_summaries.file-organization: The codebase organizes seven top-level packages (`analytics/`, `api/`, `clients/`, `core/`, `grpc/`, `proto/`, `services/`, `utils/`) with `analytics/core/` subdivided by functional concern (`infra/`, `models/`, `registry/`, `query/`, `execution/`) and `api/data_service_models/` using underscore-prefixed filenames as a deliberate private namespacing pattern, with dependency rules enforced via inline comments and `__init__.py` re-export hubs exposing public APIs via explicit `__all__` lists.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Documentation does not detail how optional dependencies are guarded in the scheduler layer, the specific implementation of the join path optimizer or composite metric execution orchestration, performance characteristics or limits of the `MetricRegistry` for large metric counts, or concrete examples of materialization config discovery beyond the `__table_type__` pattern.
section_summaries.naming-patterns: Module-level loggers follow `logger = get_module_logger(__name__)` convention, constants use `_SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` for private module-level values, error codes are `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` strings (not integers), scheduler internals are prefixed with `_` (e.g., `_base.py`, `_circuit_breaker.py`), and exception classes carry structured attributes as instance variables for programmatic consumption rather than only string messages.

autom8y::autom8y-data::design-constraint.domain_summary: The autom8y-data codebase has undergone partial refactoring to decompose monolithic components (AnalyticsEngine god-class, metrics library, materializer) into specialized modules, but several tensions persist between extracted layers and their orchestrators due to incomplete protocol-based dependency injection and backward-compatibility constraints. Key architectural concerns include bidirectional coupling between analytics and API routes, hidden parameter tunneling through kwargs, and dual execution paths for batch insight processing that block full modernization.

section_summaries.tension-catalog-completeness: materializer.py spans 3,038 lines combining error types, manifest models (TableManifest, SyncManifest), sync configuration classes (TableSyncConfig, RIJoinPair), and MaterializationJob orchestrator, with models referenced from scheduler and admin routes creating tight cross-concern coupling.
section_summaries.trade-off-documentation: analytics/routes cohabits with analytics logic for development speed; bidirectional coupling with api/ was tolerated within the same codebase but creates

autom8y::autom8y-data::feat/index.domain_summary: The autom8y-data::feat/index domain documents a feature census of 28 analytics platform capabilities across 7 categories, with 22 recommended for comprehensive documentation generation and 6 for skipping. This knowledge base captures architectural decisions, implementation patterns, and user-facing surfaces for a high-complexity data analytics platform built on DuckDB, Parquet materialization, and composite metric systems.

section_summaries.analytics-engine: 2,377-line async facade class serving as primary query API entry point with 10+ implementation files and multiple decision records (ADR-0001 through ADR-0012).
section_summaries.analytics-health-scoring: 6+ file health score service computing AHI/CHI domain-specific formulas from entity resolution, metric fetching, and industry benchmark lookups.
section_summaries.batch-insight-execution: Dual-path batch execution (BatchInsightExecutor and UnifiedBatchExecutor) mounted at separate endpoints representing documented design tension (TENSION-002).
section_summaries.connection-routing: Three-mode DuckDB connection router (LOCAL/ATTACH/HYBRID) preferring Parquet-backed 8.7ms queries with MySQL ATTACH fallback (4,821ms path) for hybrid execution.
section_summaries.crud-entity-layer: 30+ ORM domain models with unified create/read/update/delete interface supporting both REST and gRPC protocols.
section_summaries.dimension-management: 7-file dimension system with time intelligence, trailing vs last period semantics, computed dimensions, and override management (ADR-0005, ADR-0012, ADR-0007).
section_summaries.grpc-api-layer: gRPC dual-protocol CRUD service wrapping protocol buffer definitions for high-performance streaming alternatives to REST endpoints.
section_summaries.insights-layer: Named insights abstraction with 8-file post-processor chain (reconciliation, coverage, SMS cost) and user-facing endpoint exposing higher-order analytics above raw metrics (ADR-0011).
section_summaries.join-graph: NetworkX DiGraph-based join path optimizer with Dijkstra's algorithm, bidirectional edges, and pathological depth protection (ADR-0002) preventing silent result corruption.
section_summaries.market-intelligence: 37+ implementation files across 11 sub-packages delivering 5 user-facing endpoints for anomaly detection, creative intelligence, budget optimization, peer ranking, and efficiency analysis.
section_summaries.materialization-pipeline: 10+ file MySQL-to-Parquet pipeline delivering 551x performance improvement via atomic symlink swaps, row-count validation, SHA256 checksums, 96-version retention, and circuit breaker patterns (ADR-0017 through ADR-0024).
section_summaries.metric-registry: 6-file registry managing 10+ formula types (DivisionFormula, SumFormula, WeightedAverageFormula) with YAML-driven composite metric composition and deprecation lifecycle contracts (ADR-0004, ADR-0010).
section_summaries.query-pipeline: 10-file SQL compilation pipeline with split-query strategy and Cartesian product prevention addressing complex join and aggregation challenges documented in ADR-0001 and ADR-0012.
section_summaries.rest-api-layer: FastAPI factory with 40+ route files (30 CRUD entities + analytics), lifespan management, middleware chain (CORS, auth, rate limiting), and 5 discovery/output decision records (ADR-0033 through ADR-0037).

autom8y::autom8y-data::release/history.domain_summary: The autom8y-data release history documents the first v0.1.0 release, which required 7 attempts and 14 hotfixes before successful deployment to AWS ECS, with cascading regressions from RF-C1 refactoring masked by sequential CI steps (ruff format → lint → mypy → semgrep). release-log | v0.1.0 (commit 07a7524) deployed to autom8y-cluster with 2 healthy ECS tasks after 2.5 hours and 7 attempts, requiring 14 hotfixes due to RF-C1 refactoring regressions across code/lint/mypy/semgrep layers and ECS deployment fixes for REDIS_URL backward-compatibility and slowapi state.

autom8y::autom8y-data::release/platform-.domain_summary: The autom8y-data repository is a Python package distributed via AWS CodeArtifact that implements platform profile functionality within the autom8y ecosystem. It uses UV for dependency management with strict CI gates (ruff, mypy, semgrep, pytest) and deploys via a three-stage cross-repo dispatch chain ending in ECS Fargate. sibling-repos-no-dependents | No downstream dependencies across seven sibling repositories (autom8y, autom8y-ads, autom8y-asana, autom8y-auth, autom8y-config, autom8y-log, autom8y-telemetry), indicating PATCH-level complexity. available-commands-justfile | Justfile provides test, lint, fmt, type-check, and coverage commands but lacks a publish target. repository-ecosystem | Python UV package published to AWS CodeArtifact at https://autom8y-696318035277.d.codeartifact.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/pypi/autom8y-python/ with authentication via UV_INDEX_AUTOM8Y_USERNAME and CodeArtifact token, requiring explicit --publish-url flag since pyproject.toml omits publish-url configuration. pipeline-chain | Three-stage dispatch chain: test.yml (autom8y-data) triggers satellite-dispatch.yml on success, which dispatches satellite-receiver.yml (autom8y) for Docker build, ECR push, ECS deployment to autom8y-cluster/autom8y-data-service on Fargate, and smoke testing. ci-gates-in-order | Sequential enforcement of ruff format/check, mypy strict typing on auth/clients/utils modules and advisory mode on full src, semgrep linting, unit pytest, and push-only integration pytest.

autom8y::autom8y-data::scar-tissue.domain_summary: This knowledge domain documents production failures and scar tissue in the autom8y data analytics codebase, cataloging 40+ identified bugs spanning metric calculation, query execution, data materialization, and deployment issues. The domain serves as a historical record of systemic problems, their fixes, and defensive patterns to prevent recurrence.

section_summaries.agent-relevance-tagging: Scars are tagged by severity and system domain: P0 for SQL injection vulnerabilities, PSEC for security/scheduling overlaps, HF for deployment/infrastructure failures, DEF for definition/calculation defects, and SCAR for structural issues in query execution and data integrity.
section_summaries.category-coverage: Failures span six primary categories: query execution (Cartesian joins, determinism), data integrity (appointments message rows, metric inflation), metric calculation (composite dependencies, window aggregation, health scores), data materialization (DuckDB quoting, schema mutation), scheduling (overlap detection, timezone comparison), and deployment infrastructure (ECS task definitions, middleware state).
section_summaries.defensive-pattern-documentation: Defensive patterns include: hard exceptions (`CartesianRiskError`), sorted iteration guarantees, explicit parameter passing (no auto-discovery), shallow dict copying across partitions, Pydantic validators for threshold ordering, and explicit quoting style comments referencing prior incidents.
section_summaries.failure-catalog-completeness: 358 commits matching fix/bug/regression/revert keywords have been analyzed, identifying 40+ scars marked with codes (SCAR-NNN, BUG-N, DEF-N, PSEC-01) including critical issues like Cartesian product metric inflation (SCAR-001), non-deterministic query ordering (SCAR-002), DuckDB backtick quoting crashes (SCAR-003), window aggregation logic corruption (SCAR-008/009), and Pydantic runtime annotation failures (SCAR-012).
section_summaries.fix-location-mapping: Fixes are embedded across the codebase: `engine.py` (line 1346 for sorted iteration), `window_aggregation.py` (lines 82-84 for skip set logic), `materializer.py` (lines 741-748, 1793 for table filters and quoting), `QueryBuilder._validate_fact_tables()` for Cartesian guards, and `appointments.py:35` for UTC datetime normalization.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: SCAR-013 (window metric vertical join incorrect path) is marked DEFERRED as no production insight currently combines window metrics with vertical dimensions, requiring fix only if such a query is added.

autom8y::autom8y-data::test-coverage.domain_summary: This knowledge domain documents test coverage analysis and testing practices for the autom8y-data analytics platform, identifying critical gaps in unit test coverage and standardizing test conventions across the codebase. The domain tracks coverage for ~2,200+ test functions across analytics engine, query compilation, rolling window aggregation, API routes, and gRPC handlers, while highlighting uncovered modules like dimension managers, join optimizers, and audit trail logging. Testing follows pytest conventions with fixture hierarchies, mock patterns, snapshot assertions, and 574 error path tests, but faces gaps in engine lifecycle tests (8 skipped), dimension isolation tests, and infrastructure monitoring modules.

section_summaries.coverage-gaps: Critical gaps exist in analytics/core/dimensions/ (no unit tests for cache/manager/overrides), analytics/core/joins/graph.py and optimizer.py (no isolated unit tests), analytics/core/infra/audit_trail.py and pool_metrics.py (zero coverage), and 8 skipped tests in test_analytics_engine.py blocking engine lifecycle coverage.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Dimension cache/manager/overrides logic lacks isolated unit tests despite indirect coverage; join graph and optimizer modules have zero unit tests; audit_trail.py and pool_metrics.py have zero test references; fixture builder logic only covered by performance tests; analytics/routes/ modules tested via API suite without dedicated analytics-layer test directory; stub test directories exist for CLI, semantic_analytics, and enrichment without implementations.
section_summaries.test-structure-summary: Test suite comprises ~2,206 test functions across 97 API files, strong coverage for analytics engine happy paths, rolling window aggregation, composite metrics, materializers, and cartesian product prevention, with 574 pytest.raises usages for error path testing.
section_summaries.testing-conventions: Tests follow pytest naming conventions (test_{subject}.py, test_{action}()), use 8-level conftest hierarchy with autouse cleanup fixtures, employ syrupy snapshot assertions in golden master tests, leverage 4,508 mock usages via unittest.mock, and execute 91 async tests under asyncio_mode="auto" without per-test decorators.

autom8y::autom8y-scheduling::architectur.domain_summary: autom8-scheduling is a standalone Python FastAPI service extracted from autom8y-data (migration TDD-SCHED-EXTRACT-001) that manages appointment booking, availability computation, and calendar synchronization for a chiropractic business scheduling domain. The codebase uses a hybrid layer + domain organization with 31 source files structured across `api/`, `models/`, `scheduling/`, and `services/` packages, built with SQLModel, Pydantic, and async MySQL via aiomysql.

section_summaries.configuration-and-environment: Configuration uses Pydantic Settings (SchedulingSettings in `config.py`) sourced from environment variables, with secrets managed via secretspec.toml; notification services depend on SendGrid and Twilio env vars loaded in NotificationConfig; GCal client lifecycle tied to app startup/shutdown in lifespan manager.
section_summaries.data-flow: HTTP requests flow through JWT auth middleware → route handler → FastAPI dependency injection (get_session, get_gcal_client) → engine/validator domain logic → SQLAlchemy async queries to MySQL (nhc_production) → JSON response, with post-commit fire-and-forget notifications (Twilio/SendGrid via NotificationEmailService/NotificationSmsService) and GCal sync dispatchers (gcal_sync.py dispatch functions).
section_summaries.dependency-graph: Internal dependencies flow strictly one-way: `api/routes/` imports domain logic from `scheduling/` and data models from `models/`, `scheduling/` imports from `models/`, and `services/` imports from both layers with no circular dependencies; external runtime dependencies include fastapi, sqlalchemy/sqlmodel, aiomysql, prometheus-client, opentelemetry instrumentation, and autom8y-* SDKs (auth, telemetry, config, logging) resolved from AWS CodeArtifact with optional autom8y-gcal for Google Calendar integration.
section_summaries.entry-points-and-public-api: The ASGI app created by `create_app()` factory in `app.py` serves uvicorn on port 5140 (dev) exposing three unauthenticated health endpoints (`/health`, `/ready`, `/health/deps`) and JWT-authenticated scheduling endpoints under `/api/v1/scheduling/` prefix including `GET /availability`, `POST /book`, `POST /cancel`, `POST /reschedule`, and appointment CRUD operations.
section_summaries.infrastructure-integration: MySQL async connectivity via aiomysql with SQLAlchemy 2.0+ ORM, OpenTelemetry SQLAlchemy instrumentation for tracing, Prometheus client for metrics, aut
section_summaries.layer-architecture: The 4-layer architecture separates presentation (`api/routes/`, `health.py`), domain business logic (`scheduling/`), data models (`models/`), and infrastructure (`services/`, `config.py`, `app.py`), with cross-cutting observability (OTel spans, Prometheus metrics) intentionally scattered across domain and presentation layers per design ticket OBS-SCHED-001.
section_summaries.package-structure: The service uses src-layout with 31 Python files organized as a hybrid layer/domain structure: `api/routes/` for HTTP endpoints, `models/` for SQLModel ORM entities and Pydantic schemas, `scheduling/` for domain logic (booking, availability, GCal sync, reminders, notifications), and `services/` for infrastructure CRUD, packaged under hatchling build backend at version 0.1.0.

autom8y::autom8y-scheduling::conventions.domain_summary: The autom8y-scheduling codebase establishes conventions for a FastAPI-based appointment scheduling domain, with organized layered architecture, strict type checking in core modules, and domain-specific error handling patterns. This knowledge base documents the structural, naming, and stylistic standards that govern code organization, error propagation, and cross-domain communication in the scheduling microservice. FILE_ORGANIZATION | Codebase follows layered package structure under `src/autom8_scheduling/` organized by functional responsibility; `models/__init__.py` uses explicit re-export barrel with `__all__`, `api/routes/__init__.py` implements router-as-module pattern with entire routes layer in one file, and `__init__.py` files follow context-specific patterns (version-only at top-level, docstring-only for services/notifications). NAMING_CONVENTIONS | Classes use `PascalCase` with domain-specific suffixes (`Engine`, `Service`, `Body`, `Settings`, `Result`, `Error`), functions use `snake_case` verbs with patterns like `get_{resource}()` for singletons and `dispatch_{resource}_{action}()` for orchestrators, constants use `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`, and domain identifiers standardize on `office_phone` (business) and `lead_phone` (patient) throughout. ERROR_HANDLING_STYLE | Service layer defines `ServiceError` hierarchy with `http_status` and `code` class attributes caught at route boundaries and translated to `HTTPException`; scheduling domain uses separate `SchedulingValidationError` for state machine violations; fire-and-forget operations use broad `except Exception: logger.exception(...)` pattern with structured log messages in `snake_case` + `extra={}` dict. TYPE_ANNOTATION_PATTERNS | All function signatures carry type annotations with `mypy strict = true` (except domain modules with `ignore_errors = true`); uses Python 3.10+ `X | Y` union syntax (not `Optional`), `Annotated[...]` for FastAPI query params, `Any` for engine returns, and `ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra="forbid")` for immutable Pydantic request bodies. IMPORT_ORGANIZATION | Ruff `isort` groups imports into standard library, third-party, and first-party (both local `autom8_scheduling.*` and `autom8y_*` SDKs); uses absolute imports for cross-package references and relative imports within `notifications/` package; `__all__` defined selectively in 6 of 31 source files, primarily for re-export barrels and public APIs. DOCUMENTATION_STYLE | Docstrings appear on all public functions and classes; `__init__.py` files carry module-level docstrings listing sub-modules or marking private scope; inline comments explain non-obvious SQLAlchemy patterns and wildcard import tolerances; no comprehensive examples or architecture diagrams present. DOMAIN_IDIOMS | `AvailabilityEngine` computes free slots via `compute_availability()`, `BookingEngine` validates and persists appointments with state machine in `write_ops.py`, `ReminderEngine` schedules notifications, `GCalOverlay` caches free-busy data with `_FreeBusyCache`, and notifications dispatch through orchestrator functions in `dispatch.py` using `NotificationSmsService` and `NotificationEmailService`.

autom8y::autom8y-scheduling::design-cons.domain_summary: The autom8y-scheduling service is a migrated appointment-booking domain that manages provider availability, bookings, and Google Calendar synchronization for a multi-provider healthcare scheduling system. The codebase maintains compatibility with a legacy MySQL schema shared with autom8y-data while operating as an independent service with relaxed type enforcement and process-local caching. STRUCTURAL-TENSIONS: slug | Migrated code suppresses mypy errors across production modules due to SQLAlchemy typing incompatibility with autom8y-data, ApiField stubs silently discard metadata validators, dual write-paths (BookingEngine and AppointmentService) have incomplete notification unification, idempotency key lookup performs full table scans without JSON index constraints, and in-memory FreeBusy cache is process-local with no cross-instance invalidation. FROZEN-AREAS: slug | The appointments table schema mirrors production MySQL with mapped column names, Business model is read-only except four scheduling configuration fields, office_phone in E.164 format serves as the implicit natural key across all tables, appointments.config JSON column coordinates state across subsystems, API route prefix /api/v1/scheduling and health endpoints are contractual, and SLOT_BLOCKING_STATUSES/CANCELLABLE_STATUSES are derived from production audit data. VERSION-AND-COMPATIBILITY-CONSTRAINTS: slug | Python 3.12+ with FastAPI 0.109+, SQLAlchemy 2.0.23+, Pydantic v2, SQLModel 0.0.14+, aiomysql 0.2+, autom8y-config 1.2.1+, autom8y-auth 1.1.0+, and autom8y-telemetry 0.6.0+ sourced from AWS CodeArtifact with uv lock using --no-sources in Docker builds. INVARIANTS-AND-CONTRACTS: slug | GCal failures never rollback MySQL bookings with appointments.event_id as sole binding, GCal overlay is subtractive-only slot removal, status transitions follow a terminal state machine, queries cannot exceed 14-day lookforward, phones must match E.164 regex, appointments are soft-deleted, atomic booking uses SELECT FOR UPDATE, list pagination caps at 100 items, idempotency keys stored in config JSON, settings cached via lru_cache singleton, cancellations verify both office_phone and lead_phone, every request gets x-request-id header, and immutable fields block updates. RISK-ZONES: slug | Booking conflict detection uses two-phase in-memory scans without SQL range predicates under concurrent load, VARCHAR datetime parsing silently skips malformed records, GCal sync commits event_id separately risking orphaned API calls, notification deduplication via JSON config is non-atomic allowing duplicates, async_sessionmaker recreated per-request instead of reused, gcal_shadow_mode defaults True risking unvalidated disables, and pool_recycle=3600 can experience transient MySQL errors despite pool_pre_ping. EXTERNAL-CONSTRAINTS: slug | MySQL schema (chiropractors, leads) owned by autom8y-data requiring coordinated changes, Google Calendar API enforces 5-minute cache rate-limit defense without retry-backoff, Twilio uses fixed /2010-04-01/ API version with 10-second timeout, SendGrid hardcoded to https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/mail/send with 2 retry attempts, and AWS CodeArtifact requires

autom8y::autom8y-scheduling::test-covera.domain_summary: This knowledge domain documents the test coverage strategy for autom8y's scheduling microservice, including a two-level test structure with 69 test functions organized by concern rather than source layout. The test suite uses pytest with async support, OTel snapshot-based regression testing for instrumentation validation, and achieves 32% source module coverage with a deliberately low 20% CI threshold reflecting the codebase's migration state. TEST-ORGANIZATION: Tests organized into 5 files with 21 classes covering health endpoints, proxy contracts, OTel instrumentation, write operations, and golden trace snapshots, using class-based structure with `async def test_...` methods and no module-scope functions. TEST-INFRASTRUCTURE: pytest configured with `asyncio_mode = "auto"`, pytest-asyncio, pytest-cov, httpx, respx, and autom8y-telemetry dependencies; coverage excludes pragmas and TYPE_CHECKING blocks with branch coverage enabled but a deliberately low 20% threshold for CI. COVERAGE-PATTERNS: 8 of 25 source modules directly tested via unit tests with mocked sessions, OTel span verification, and golden snapshots; `booking.py`, `engine.py`, `gcal_overlay.py` use instrumentation testing while `write_ops.py` uses pure unit tests and `health.py` uses integration testing against a mocked FastAPI app. TEST-GAPS: High-risk gaps include untested `AppointmentService` status updates, 11 untested ASGI route handlers, and untested `ReminderEngine` and `check_scheduling_readiness`; medium-risk gaps cover notification templates and SMS/email service logic; low-risk gaps include model definitions and configuration loading. FIXTURE-AND-MOCK-PATTERNS: Top-level `app` fixture creates FastAPI with bypassed lifespan, `client` fixture provides async ASGI transport, and `golden_traces/conftest.py` provides fresh `TracerProvider` per test with direct monkeypatching of module-level singletons; dominant mock strategy uses direct attribute assignment for stubbing over factory libraries. TEST-QUALITY-SIGNALS: Positive signals include frozen snapshot types, explicit typed OTel span attribute regression guards, side-effect event verification, comprehensive docstrings, and integration test marking; weaknesses include zero parameterized tests, no property-based testing, boilerplate duplication in sprint2 tests, and no ASGI integration tests for route handlers despite available fixtures. KNOWLEDGE-GAPS: Actual measured coverage percentage unknown without running `pytest --cov`, CI test output unavailable for inspection, external `autom8y-telemetry` convention check gate criteria unobservable, and detailed coverage gaps for validation, reminder, appointment service, and model modules inferred from test import absence.

autom8y::autom8y-sms::architecture.domain_summary: autom8y SMS is a Python 3.12 Lambda-based SMS conversation service for client leads built as a "Zapier replacement," deployed on AWS EventBridge schedule with a Twilio Webhook → Lambda → Claude API → Twilio API architecture and autom8y_data for message persistence. The codebase uses Pydantic models, circuit-breaker protected clients, and a hub-and-spoke orchestrator pattern with strict downward dependency flow from handlers through services to leaf packages (config, models, prompts, tools).

section_summaries.data-flow: Inbound SMS webhook triggers Lambda → `process_pending_conversations()` polls data service → `ConversationOrchestrator.process_conversation()` builds context (lead, business, address, messages) → prompt selected per lead status → Claude API called with tools enabled → tool_use validated against availability/temporal/business-hour constraints → tool result formatted and fed back to Claude → final text response sent via Twilio → all messages logged with instrumentation fields (prompt_version, token counts, model_id); separate reminder orchestrator runs on 5-minute schedule to send appointment reminders.
section_summaries.entry-points-and-api-surface: Two production Lambda handlers (client_lead on 1-minute schedule, appointment_reminder on 5-minute schedule), CLI tool `sms-console` with stub clients for interactive development, plugin entry point for observability via `autom8y_devx.narrative_rules`, and core public APIs: `ConversationOrchestrator.process_conversation()`, `ReminderOrchestrator.run()`, `SmsDataClient`/`TwilioClient` factories, and global `get_config()` singleton.
section_summaries.key-abstractions: `ConversationContext` aggregates lead/business/address data with Pydantic models; `ConversationOrchestrator` manages SMS response pipeline (context fetch → prompt selection → Claude tool dispatch loop → send); `SmsDataClient` and `TwilioClient` wrap external APIs with circuit breakers; tools package defines five scheduling schemas (check_availability, book, cancel, get_appointments, reschedule) with server-side validation rules (SD-03/04/05) and formatting functions; prompts layer has two system prompt versions (sdr-v2 for prospects, scheduled-v2 for existing appointments).
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Console module structure and interactive session loop details; exact schema of autom8y_data messages and lead endpoints; specific Anthropic Claude model version and token limits; business rules for pilot office phone filtering and per-conversation 45-second timeout; complete narrative rule set for observability (22 rules mentioned but not detailed); exception handling and retry strategies in orchestrators.
section_summaries.layer-boundaries: Strict downward-only import graph enforced: handlers → services/orchestrator (hub) → clients → models/prompts/tools (leaf packages with no internal imports), with TYPE_CHECKING guards to prevent circular dependencies and narrative/ as cross-cutting concern via Python entry points.
section_summaries.package-structure: Source tree organized under `src/autom8_sms/` with handlers (Lambda entry points), services (orchestrators), clients (adapters), models (Pydantic domain types), prompts (Claude system prompts), tools (AI tool definitions and validation), config (environment settings), and console/narrative/debug utilities, using Hatchling build system and entry point `sms-console`.

autom8y::autom8y-sms::conventions.domain_summary: The autom8y-sms codebase is a Python 3.12 SMS conversation platform built with uv/hatchling and enforced by ruff (100-char lines) and mypy strict mode. It implements SMS appointment reminders and lead conversation routing via AWS Lambda, using structured logging, OTEL tracing, and security-by-design patterns with server-side validation.

section_summaries.domain-specific-idioms: Clients and orchestrators expose `from_env()` classmethods as the canonical cold-start construction path, implement `__aenter__/__aexit__` async context managers for resource cleanup, use `@dataclass` for internal computation results and `BaseModel` for wire contracts and settings, employ `TYPE_CHECKING` guards for circular import avoidance, and enforce server-side phone number resolution as a security pattern (SD-03) with `assert` preconditions in tool handlers.
section_summaries.error-handling-style: The codebase uses custom exception classes per integration boundary (e.g., `TwilioError` with numeric `code` field), employs catch-at-boundary pattern at Lambda handlers returning `{"statusCode": 500, "body": {"error": str(e)}}`, and uses result-type dataclasses (`ProcessingResult`, `ValidationResult`) to avoid exception propagation in orchestrator flows, with fault-isolation loops for batch processing.
section_summaries.file-organization: The package separates concerns strictly: `config.py` for settings, `models/` for domain Pydantic models, `clients/` for HTTP clients and custom exceptions, `services/` for orchestrators with business logic, `handlers/` for Lambda entry points only, `tools/` split into schemas (`definitions.py`), serializers (`formatting.py`), typed shapes (`models.py`), and guards (`validation.py`), and `prompts/` and `narrative/` for constants and Devx plugins.
