autom8y::knossos::feat/explain-command.domain_summary: Zero-dependency offline concept glossary providing discoverability for knossos mythology domain vocabulary, with harness-agnostic bridging and project-aware live context counts. Resolves TENSION-015 by extracting concept package from CLI layer, enabling internal/search to access domain terminology independently.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Panic-on-init for malformed files enables build-time detection, context functions hardcode ClaudeChannel causing incorrect counts for Gemini projects, graceful degradation on unreadable sessions directory, Levenshtein-only matching without NLP, and 6 concepts lacking context functions.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: 16 embedded YAML-fronted concepts with exact/alias/Levenshtein-distance-<=3 lookup chain, 10 context-injection functions reading project state, and dual output modes (list/lookup) displaying "{name} (harness_term)" format.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Registry implemented in internal/concept/concept.go with LookupConcept/AllConcepts/levenshtein functions, 16 embedded .md files in concepts/, CLI in internal/cmd/explain/ (explain.go, context.go with 10 contextFuncs), and search integration via CollectConcepts feeding ari ask index.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: potnia.md references non-existent pythia concept in see_also, contextSOS implementation only checks directory existence, and pythia concept file missing despite codebase references.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Zero-dependency, offline glossary for knossos mythology terminology with harness_term bridging and live project state contextualization, extracted to decouple concept data from CLI layer.

autom8y::knossos::feat/hook-infrastructu.domain_summary: Live interception layer over AI harness lifecycle events implementing seven platform behaviors (context protection, session automation, audit trail, compaction, budget tracking, drift detection, code hygiene) with Go binary and canonical vocabulary (ADR-0032) shared between CC and Gemini as translation peers. Fail-open architecture with 100ms timeout budget ensures resilience while maintaining audit integrity through events.jsonl and clew contract. CONCEPTUAL-MODEL | LifecycleAdapter pattern translates 18 canonical lifecycle events via ClaudeAdapter and GeminiAdapter to uniform Env struct, with envelope pattern (SCAR-009) reading permissionDecision from hookSpecificOutput and clew contract supporting coexistent v2/v3 events.jsonl formats registered via config/hooks.yaml. IMPLEMENTATION-MAP | Hook system spans internal/hook/ (8 files handling stdin parsing, adapters, event/output/input/auth), internal/cmd/hook/ (18+ handler binaries for writeguard, agentguard, autopark, clew, budget, driftdetect, etc.), and internal/hook/clewcontract/ (20 files for event system). BOUNDARIES-AND-FAILURE-MODES | All handlers default to allow on error except auth failure which explicitly denies; 100ms/500ms Go timeout (inner) precedes harness timeout (outer); async hooks (clew, suggest, driftdetect) are non-blocking; ClaudeAdapter defaults when KNOSSOS_CHANNEL unset; legacy and CC-native output formats coexist with O(n) EventWriter overhead. KNOWLEDGE-GAPS | context.go full implementation unread; ADR-0002 and ADR-0011 absent from disk; hook registration materialization path (BuildHooksSettings) not traced. PURPOSE-AND-DESIGN-RATIONALE | Live interception layer providing fail-open lifecycle event handling with 100ms performance budget; ADR-0032 establishes knossos-owned canonical vocabulary (pre_tool, run_shell) making CC and Gemini translation peers.

autom8y::knossos::feat/index.domain_summary: Knossos is the Autom8y platform's core feature census comprising 46 features across 7 categories for code generation decisions, with heavy emphasis on session management, materialization pipelines, and AI agent orchestration. The domain covers end-to-end workflow automation including session lifecycle FSMs, file provenance tracking, hook-based event systems, and multi-channel LLM integration patterns.

section_summaries.agent-scaffolding: 27 implementation files with archetype templates, MCP dependency declaration, schema validation, dual-channel adapters (Claude + Gemini), and factory pattern ADR-0024.
section_summaries.artifact-registry: .ledge/ artifact aggregation with phase/type filtering, auto-promotion
section_summaries.hook-infrastructure: 41 implementation files spanning 14+ hook subcommands (write-guard, agent-guard, clew, session-end, budget, subagent, git-conventions) with stdin JSON transport, lifecycle event coverage, and Go migration from bash.
section_summaries.inscription-system: CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md marker parser with HTML comment syntax, region ownership model, 2-axis context binding, and 4-subcommand CLI (sync, diff, validate, rollback) across 21 implementation files.
section_summaries.materialization-pipeline: Dominant 53+ file hub package orchestrating rite resolution, materialization via mena routing, CLAUDE.md inscription, and idempotent WriteIfChanged pattern with 6-tier rite discovery and 5 sub-packages.
section_summaries.mena-system: File-extension-based routing (.dro.md → commands, .lego.md → skills) with 16 implementation files, scope filtering, collision namespace logic, and 3 ADRs establishing dromena/legomena convention.
section_summaries.provenance-system: File divergence/collision detection and manifest-based tracking preventing user content overwrites, with 8 implementation files and unified provenance model ADR-0026.
section_summaries.rite-management: 25 implementation files managing 19 embedded rites (10x-dev, arch, clinic, debt-triage, docs, etc.) with manifest-based invocation, budget calculator, state machine, and 6+ user-facing CLI subcommands.
section_summaries.session-event-system: Unified event schema (events.jsonl) with dual-format bridge logic, cross-cutting orchestrator handoff events, and ADR-0027 establishing consistent event contract across session, sails, and hook packages.
section_summaries.session-forking: Fray subcommand enabling parallel session strands with dedicated context fields (FrayedFrom/Strands) and ADR-0006 parallel orchestration pattern.
section_summaries.session-lifecycle: FSM-based session management with 22-file internal package, 41 CLI subcommands (create, park, resume, wrap, fray, gc, audit, lock, snapshot, migrate, recover), and 3 foundational ADRs covering state machine redesign and unified event sourcing.
section_summaries.validation-framework: 20 implementation + schema files with 11 JSON schemas (adr, agent, handoff-criteria, session-context, white-sails) and embedded schema validation against artifact types.
section_summaries.worktree-management: Git worktree lifecycle integration with session seeding via .knossos/ state, 19 implementation files, 10 subcommands (create, sync, remove, clone, export, import), and 2 ADRs covering environment contract.

autom8y::knossos::feat/inscription-syste.domain_summary: The inscription system generates and maintains AI harness context files (CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md) using region-based ownership where knossos regions are template-controlled, satellite regions preserve user content, and regenerate regions rebuild from live project state. It employs HTML comment markers, SHA-256 hashing for edit detection, and a KNOSSOS_MANIFEST.yaml file to track configuration across multiple channels via template branching.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Malformed markers cause ParseError extraction failures; hash mismatches silently overwrite on first sync; double-wrap prevented via isWrapped() checks; deprecated regions avoid zombie adoption through static+dynamic validation; pipeline hardcodes .claude/CLAUDE.md path (TENSION-013); template failures silently skip non-satellite regions; no concurrent sync locking; legacy backups use fixed filenames.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Nine default sections managed by MarkerParser (line scanner with code-block escaping), Generator (Go templates with Sprig and 4 custom functions), and Merger (ownership-respecting merge with double-wrap detection), tracked in KNOSSOS_MANIFEST.yaml with region metadata and two deprecated regions (slash-commands v18, navigation v20).
section_summaries.implementation-map: Eight files in internal/inscription/ (types.go, marker.go, manifest.go, generator.go, merger.go, sync.go, pipeline.go, backup.go), six cmd files for sync/diff/validate/rollback/backups operations, and eight .md.tpl template files in knossos/templates/sections/.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: ADR-0021 not located on disk; materialization pipeline integration depth inferred from architecture documentation; conditional region evaluation implementation status unclear.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Generates AI harness context with three ownership types (knossos/satellite/regenerate), uses HTML comment markers for invisible region delimiting, detects user edits via SHA-256 hashes, prevents file watcher triggers with writeIfChanged, and supports legacy migration and multi-channel rendering via RenderContext.Channel.

autom8y::knossos::feat/know-system.domain_summary: The Codebase Knowledge Domain System (.know/) is a pre-computed, cached knowledge management system for monorepos that balances freshness through multi-signal staleness detection (time, code changes, dependencies, land integration) with incremental regeneration via ChangeManifest and SemanticDiff. It uses qualified domain names (org::repo::domain), upward filesystem walks for nearest-wins merging, and path-prefixed namespacing to handle monorepo complexity while maintaining referential integrity.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Git dependency with 30s timeouts and graceful degradation; custom matchScope handles ** via splitting rather than full glob; freshness false-negatives when working directory differs from repo root; dependency graph assumes qualified names match plain lookups; validation regex produces false positives on renamed PascalCase types; incremental cycle exhaustion risk when ForceFull=true without Fresh=false; land hash degrades gracefully on deleted files.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Domain structure uses QualifiedDomainName (org::repo::domain format), five orthogonal DomainStatus staleness signals, ChangeManifest for categorized file lists with delta ratio thresholding (0.5 or 5000 lines), SemanticDiff for AST-level Go declaration changes, and ValidationReport for reference integrity checking across file paths, function names, and commit hashes.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Six internal/know/ files implement core logic: know.go (meta reading, staleness detection, dependency DAG), manifest.go (change computation and mode recommendations), validate.go (4-type reference verification), qualified.go (domain name parsing), astdiff.go (go/ast-based file diffing), discover.go (service boundary discovery); CLI via knows.go with list, --check, --validate, --delta, --semantic-diff, --discover, --scope-dir commands and unscoped manifest caching.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Three test files not analyzed: knows_test.go, validate_test.go, and astdiff_test.go; KnowledgeIndex integration with QualifiedDomainName not fully traced.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Pre-computes durable codebase knowledge with three pillars: freshness via source_scope/source_hash/expires_after dual staleness signals, incremental regeneration through update_mode/incremental_cycle/ChangeManifest/SemanticDiff, and monorepo awareness via FindKnowDirs upward walk and nearest-wins merge with path-prefixed domain names.

autom8y::knossos::feat/manifest-operatio.domain_summary: General-purpose toolkit for inspecting, validating, comparing, and reconciling YAML/JSON manifest files with advisory (non-blocking) validation, Git ref support, and three-way merge with four strategies. Provides uniform manifest envelope handling, recursive diff with jQuery-style paths, and distinct from internal/materialize's RiteManifest implementation.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: internal/manifest is NOT used by internal/materialize (separate structs), full JSON Schema validation disabled (compiler nil), ValidateRiteManifest only warns, format detection bug in merge --write-to compares path string to "yaml" literal, ValidateBytes panics on nil compiler, no CLI-layer tests.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Manifest uses uniform envelope (Path, Format, Content map, Raw bytes) with auto-detected format, Git ref detection excluding Windows drive letters, four merge strategies with field-level conflict markers, recursive diff tree walk with set semantics via --ignore-order, and lightweight schema validation with disabled jsonschema compiler.
section_summaries.implementation-map: internal/manifest/ contains manifest.go (Load/Save/Clone/Get with advisory ValidateRiteManifest), diff.go (Diff/FormatUnified), merge.go (4 strategies with conflict markers), schema.go (SchemaValidator/DetectSchemaFromPath); internal/cmd/manifest/ provides show/validate/diff/merge commands with --strict/--write-to/--dry-run flags and 3 embedded schema files.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: rite-manifest.schema.json requires agents[].file but Go validator skips it, schema and operative struct architecturally diverged, and agent-manifest.schema.json relationship is unclear.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Rite manifest validation is advisory (TD-3), supports Git refs (HEAD:.claude/manifest.json), implements three-way merge with smart/ours/theirs/union strategies, and maintains separation from internal/materialize's RiteManifest package.

autom8y::knossos::feat/materialization-p.domain_summary: Rite Materialization Pipeline converts rite source definitions into channel-ready configuration directories (.claude/ or .gemini/) with three core invariants: idempotency via writeIfChanged guards, user content preservation across satellite regions, and harness agnosticism through the ChannelCompiler interface. Provenance tracking uses SHA-256 checksums and orphan detection stored in .knossos/, while embedded assets enable single-binary distribution. knowledge-gaps | procession sub-package, user-scope mena sync details, inscription merge pipeline, and ADR-0016 remain unverified or missing from codebase review. purpose-and-design-rationale | Three non-negotiable invariants enforce idempotency to prevent CC file watcher crashes, preserve user content in satellite regions and user agents, and maintain harness agnosticism while isolating provenance in .knossos/ outside the context window. conceptual-model | Three-scope architecture (Rite/Org/User) with 6-tier source resolution (explicit > project > user > org > platform > embedded), three agent tiers (standing/rite/summonable), Mena priority weighting, and collision checker prevents user-scope shadowing of rite-owned resources. implementation-map | MaterializeWithOptions() executes a 20+ step pipeline in `internal/materialize/materialize.go` including resolution, validation, orphan detection, hook/skill/MCP resolution, and agent/Mena/rules/inscription materialization, with ClaudeCompiler pass-through and GeminiCompiler applying TOML command transforms and key stripping. boundaries-and-failure-modes | Fatal failures include source resolution, manifest parse, and CLAUDE.md pre-validation errors; non-fatal failures trigger divergence detection and scope-limited fallback; idempotency guaranteed by writeIfChanged with no pre-delete, non-transactional pipeline design, and selective writes from managed-set only.

autom8y::knossos::feat/mena-lint.domain_summary: Mena and Agent Lint is a pre-sync validation gate for source artifacts addressing three critical SCARs: silent @skill-name reference ignoring (SCAR-017), invalid agent color dropping (SCAR-019), and session artifacts in shared mena (SCAR-027). It operates exclusively on source files rather than materialized output, with --check=preferential-language as the only non-zero exit path, and uses four severity levels (CRIT/HIGH/MED/LOW) to categorize structural, convention, and style violations.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Skips materialized .claude/ output and user-scope agents; expectedForkState allowlist staleness; no --fail-on-critical flag; preferential-language word-boundary regex false positives on "claude"/"gemini"; mena.Walk skips embedded FS; session-artifact exempts examples/ with no color/size/turns test coverage.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Four severity levels (CRIT/HIGH/MED/LOW) with specialized rules for agents (9 rules including color validation, maxTurns deviation, skill syntax), dromena (fork-task-conflict SCAR-018, workflow invocability), legomena (triggers, sizing), and cross-cutting concerns (session-artifact, preferential-language harness-agnosticism).
section_summaries.implementation-map: Four-file codebase in internal/cmd/lint/ (lint.go 1141 lines with all rules/allowlist, lint_preferential.go 209 lines for harness scans, 879 combined test lines) using internal/mena.Walk and internal/frontmatter.Parse.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Census references non-existent "models" file; exit code design (informational vs gate) undocumented; no --check support for individual standard rules beyond preferential-language.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Pre-sync validation gate addressing SCAR-017 (@skill-name silent ignoring), SCAR-019 (invalid agent colors), and SCAR-027 (session artifacts in shared mena) with --check=preferential-language as sole non-zero exit mechanism.

autom8y::knossos::feat/mena-system.domain_summary: The Mena distribution system routes dromena (transient commands) and legomena (persistent skills) through file-extension-based routing (.dro.md/.lego.md) to eliminate frontmatter inspection, using a two-level package architecture (internal/mena for primitives, internal/materialize/mena for full projection) with priority-ordered source resolution and namespace flattening for user-friendly invocation. The system enables progressive disclosure through INDEX-file patterns with companion files while maintaining content lifecycle separation.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Mixed dro/lego directories block skill resolution; walk doesn't support embedded FS; namespace collisions fall back to directory-path routing with warning; stale cleanup requires provenance tracking; standalone files default to "dro" type; compilers apply only to primary files.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Six-pass projection pipeline (collect, namespace resolve, flatten names, write with transforms, clean stale, reconcile untracked) supports two modes (destructive rite-scoped with stale wipe, additive user-scoped preservation) and four-level source priority with three entry structures (directory INDEX, standalone file, grouping directory).
section_summaries.implementation-map: Leaf package internal/mena (types, source, exists, walk) feeds hub internal/materialize/mena (types, frontmatter, collect, namespace, engine, walker, transform, content_rewrite) via materialize_mena.go integration; MenaFrontmatter schema includes name, description, argument-hint, triggers, allowed-tools, model, with ChannelCompiler interface for harness-specific output (TOML for Gemini).
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Three critical gaps: renderProcessionMena rendering details untraced, ADRs 0021/0023/0025 not found on disk, and Gemini channel compiler TOML format not documented.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Extension-based routing (.dro.md/.lego.md) eliminates frontmatter inspection, with two-level package architecture (leaf primitives, hub projection) and bottom-up priority resolution (active rite > dependency > shared > procession > platform).

autom8y::knossos::feat/naxos-orphan-clea.domain_summary: Naxos is a report-only session cleanup tool for long-running projects that identifies orphaned sessions through a two-stage pipeline (scan with 3 predicates + triage with 4 severity levels) and surfaces actionable recommendations without performing mutations. Named after the island where Theseus abandoned Ariadne, it generates the NAXOS_TRIAGE.md artifact optimized for <5ms consumption by hooks via ReadTriageSummary.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Report-only with no mutations; reads only SESSION_CONTEXT.md and WHITE_SAILS.yaml (not events.jsonl); corrupt sessions silently skipped; empty sails treated as GRAY; inactive threshold strictly >; --no-artifact flag has naming inversion (writeArtifact variable means skipArtifact when true).
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Three orphan predicates (INACTIVE >24h, INCOMPLETE_WRAP in wrap phase, STALE_SAILS >7d parked) feed into four severity levels (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) with DELETE only recommended for age >30d, first matching predicate wins.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Five files in `internal/naxos/` (types.go, scanner.go with 3 predicates, triage.go, artifact.go with ReadTriageSummary, report.go/triage_report.go) plus CLI in `internal/cmd/naxos/` and dromenon at `/naxos` in mena/session/naxos/INDEX.dro.md.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Three gaps exist: session.LoadContext parse failure behavior on missing status field unknown; hook wiring for naxos_summary injection not traced; paths.IsSessionDir pattern not read.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Addresses session accumulation in long-running projects with a report-only design (refuses auto-archive/delete) using a two-stage pipeline: scan with 3 predicates plus triage with 4 severity levels, producing NAXOS_TRIAGE.md artifact for hook consumption optimized for <5ms.

autom8y::knossos::feat/org-management.domain_summary: Organization-Level Resource Management enables multiple projects to share rites, agents, and mena across repositories by separating resource materialization (copied to ~/.claude/ at sync time) from knowledge registry (cross-repo domain catalog for Clew). The system resolves active organization via KNOSSOS_ORG env var or config file, storing org resources in user channel directories for cross-project availability.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Org scope does not affect project-scope resources, registry sync decouples from resource sync, flat files only with subdirectories silently skipped, missing org config results in non-fatal skip status, and ActiveOrg caches via sync.Once requiring test cleanup via ResetKnossosHome.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Three-tier architecture (Config with active-org pointer, Data with org resources/metadata, Registry with cross-repo catalog) uses OrgContext interface with qualified names (org::repo::domain) and fires at Phase 1.5 in the sync pipeline with ORG_PROVENANCE_MANIFEST.yaml tracking.
section_summaries.implementation-map: CLI commands in internal/cmd/org/, config/OrgContext in internal/config/, paths helpers (OrgDataDir, OrgRitesDir, OrgAgentsDir, OrgMenaDir) in internal/paths/paths.go, sync logic in internal/materialize/orgscope/sync.go (flat files only), and registry management in internal/registry/org/ (DomainCatalog, SyncRegistry, LoadCatalog/SaveCatalog).
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Unconfirmed: collision detection between org/user scope, procession org integration traces, and ari sync --org flag path passing behavior.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Org scope materializes shared resources to XDG data while registry/org maintains a cross-repo domain catalog, with active org resolved by KNOSSOS_ORG env var or config file.

autom8y::knossos::feat/project-initializ.domain_summary: **Domain Summary** The `ari init` command bootstraps new Knossos projects with two scaffolding modes (minimal and rite), using embedded assets for single-binary distribution and version-controlled XDG extraction. It implements idempotent initialization with non-Knossos `.claude/` directory guards, surgical gitignore management, and project directory structure creation (.knossos/, .sos/, .ledge/).

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Channel hardcoded to .claude, hooks.yaml is write-once (no version upgrade), XDG extraction is best-effort with warnings, mkdir failures are silent, corrupted KNOSSOS_MANIFEST propagates errors, and KeepOrphans=true preserves stale artifacts on force re-initialization.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Three initialization modes (already initialized no-op, MaterializeMinimal, or Sync with ScopeAll + KeepOrphans) scaffold .knossos/, .sos/, .ledge/ hierarchies with version-sentinel controlled XDG extraction and surgical gitignore Knossos marker block replacement.
section_summaries.implementation-map: `init.go::runInit` orchestrates project dir resolution, idempotency checks, non-knossos guards, materializer construction with embedded FS, hooks.yaml bootstrap, XDG extraction, and scaffolding branches to minimal or rite sync, with 10+ test scenarios covering fresh dirs, rite mode, force flag, and gitignore edge cases.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Three gaps: --source flag behavior on non-existent paths untraced, config/hooks.yaml content not examined, and no user-facing documentation beyond --help exists.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Bootstraps projects into knossos workflow as the only command with SetNeedsProject(false,false), supporting minimal scaffold (CLAUDE.md + settings + directories) or rite scaffold (full ari sync) with non-knossos .claude/ guards, version-sentinel controlled XDG mena extraction, and embedded-asset single-binary distribution.

autom8y::knossos::feat/project-status-da.domain_summary: **Domain Summary:** This feature implements a read-only project health dashboard (`ari status`) that aggregates status from five directory trees (.claude/, .knossos/, .know/, .ledge/, .sos/) to provide unified diagnostics for session initialization and debugging. The dashboard exits with code 1 on unhealthy status (when channel directory missing), making it suitable as a CI gate, and uses minimal hand-written YAML scanning to avoid heavy dependencies.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Hardcoded to ClaudeChannel (Gemini-only projects show unhealthy); displays only Fresh bool without staleness reason; no per-agent details; collectKnow silently returns zero counts on error; malformed session contexts silently excluded from counts.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Five collectors aggregate: Channel (active rite, agent count, last sync), Knossos (satellite rite inventory), Know (domain freshness via ReadMeta), Ledge (artifact counts by category), SOS (session counts by status) using minimal hand-written YAML scanner for session status reading.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Single file `internal/cmd/status/status.go` contains NewStatusCmd with five collectors (collectChannel, collectKnossos, collectKnow, collectLedge, collectSOS) plus readSessionStatus streaming scanner and formatAge helper; tested in status_test.go.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: YAML rendering path unverified; know.ReadMeta error handling may be unintentional; archived count uses directory name heuristic with potential overcount risk.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Read-only unified health overview of five directory trees with single diagnostic screen; healthy state requires channel directory existence; returns exit code 1 for CI-gate usability.

autom8y::knossos::feat/provenance-system.domain_summary: File Provenance Tracking system for autom8y::knossos tracks file ownership across shared channel directories using three axes (Owner/Scope/Source) with dual manifests to prevent cross-contamination between rite and user scopes. The system detects user divergence from knossos-owned files, applies a 4-step merge algorithm, and enforces one-way leaf package constraints to maintain structural integrity.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: LoadOrBootstrap fails-closed on corrupt manifests (LB-009), volatile files excluded from tracking, deleted files promoted with empty checksums, OwnerType/SourceType strings require manual sync with materialize/source tier.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: DetectDivergence pipeline promotes knossos files to user on edit detection, 4-step Merge algorithm carries forward knossos entries, layers divergence/collector promotions, skips user-promoted files unless --overwrite-diverged, and handles multi-channel manifests (claude vs GEMINI).
section_summaries.implementation-map: Five-file internal/provenance package (provenance.go, manifest.go, collector.go, merge.go, divergence.go) with CLI via ari provenance show, sha256:+64hex checksum format, and collector parameter integration at every materialize stage.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: ADR-0026 unavailable, --overwrite-diverged CLI wiring unconfirmed, census references non-existent scanner.go/report.go files.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Three-axis provenance model (Owner/Scope/Source) with separate PROVENANCE_MANIFEST.yaml manifests per channel prevents cross-contamination, uses structural equality comparison to avoid triggering CC file watchers (LB-002), and provides v1→v2.0 migration path.

autom8y::knossos::feat/rite-management.domain_summary: Rite Management is knossos's organizational unit system enabling composable bundles of agents, skills, hooks, and workflows across four forms (simple, practitioner, procedural, full), with active rite replacement via ari sync and bounded invocation through token budget guardrails (default 50K). The system uses a four-tier context resolution chain (project > user > org > platform > embedded) and tracks state via manifest.yaml, .knossos/ACTIVE_RITE, and INVOCATION_STATE.yaml. CONCEPTUAL-MODEL | Rite manifests define name, entry_agent, phases, agents, dromena/legomena, dependencies, complexity_levels, hooks, and mcp_servers; active rite state persists in .knossos/ACTIVE_RITE while invocation state tracks borrowings and budget in INVOCATION_STATE.yaml; context.yaml enables key-value injection into inscriptions across 19 bundled rites. IMPLEMENTATION-MAP | internal/rite/ module (10 files) implements RiteManifest validation, four-tier enumeration discovery, 11-step borrow pipeline invocation, InvocationState persistence, BudgetCalculator, workflow execution, and four-tier context resolution; internal/cmd/rite/ provides 10 subcommands (list, info, current, invoke, release, status, validate, context, pantheon) with Syncer interface for dependency inversion. BOUNDARIES-AND-FAILURE-MODES | Rite management (internal/rite) does not write channel directories—Syncer interface defers materialization; invocation differs from materialization with CLAUDE.md injection deferred to Phase 2; missing ACTIVE_RITE throws CodeFileNotFound; invocation conflicts raise ErrBorrowConflict; budget estimates default to 50K tokens; context chain silently shadows resolutions; loadRite silently skips invalid rites. KNOWLEDGE-GAPS | ari sync --rite full switching internals untraced, dependency resolution for shared rites through materialize untraced, ADR-0007 missing from disk.

autom8y::knossos::feat/session-event-sys.domain_summary: Unified Session Event System for resolving dual-emitter schema collision between session.EventEmitter (v1) and clewcontract.BufferedEventWriter (v2/v3), with v3 TypedEvent providing structured per-type payloads. System maintains write-path unification via clewcontract while preserving read-path backward compatibility across three event format generations for audit compliance (LB-004). Implements 29+ event types across six categories with async buffered writing (5s flush), structured decision recording via Stamp, and type-to-source inference.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: BufferedEventWriter silently drops events post-Close(), loses data in 5s crash window, re-queues on flush failure tracking only last error; read path silently skips malformed lines and misreads on wrong detection order; triggers read v2 only (v3 zero-valued); type_rename.go is immutable append-only; context_switch uses non-dotted legacy value; field.updated and hook.fired lack producers.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: System spans three event format generations (v1 Event with "event" field, v2 clewcontract.Event with "type" field, v3 TypedEvent with "data" field and highest detection precedence), 29+ event types across six categories, dual writer strategies (sync EventWriter vs async BufferedEventWriter with 5s flush), append-only v2-v3 rename map, three EventSources (cli/hook/agent), and Stamp for structured decision recording with four trigger types.
section_summaries.implementation-map: `internal/hook/clewcontract/` (20 files) provides v2/v3 event definitions, 29 per-type data structs, dual writers, RecordToolEvent integration, append-only rename map, type-to-source inference, and trigger logic; read bridge in `internal/session/events_read.go` normalizes v1/v2/v3 from single JSONL via detection order (v3 data → v1 event → v2 type).
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: ADR-0027 document not found at docs/decisions/, SESSION-1 spec missing from repository, ari hook clew command internals not reviewed.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Solves schema collision between incompatible dual emitters (session.EventEmitter SCREAMING_CASE vs clewcontract.BufferedEventWriter snake_case dotted) by converging on clewcontract as canonical write type (ADR-0027), deprecating session.EventEmitter, and introducing three-format read bridge for audit backward compatibility.

autom8y::knossos::feat/session-forking.domain_summary: Session forking (Fray) enables parallel workstreams within a single initiative by creating child sessions that inherit parent context while allowing independent work, with automatic parent parking and strand lifecycle tracking (ACTIVE -> LANDED). The feature uses a directed parent-child graph model with worktree isolation, polymorphic YAML deserialization for backward compatibility, and non-fatal event emission (session.frayed, session.strand_resolved).

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: FSM constrains fray only from ACTIVE->PARKED state, enforces exclusive parent lock with 5-minute stale threshold, removes child directory on parent save failure, leaves worktree cleanup unhandled, never sets FrameRef on Strand, and lacks ADR-0006 reference.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Implements directed parent-child graph where parents transition to PARKED with strand lists, children reference FrayPoint phase and FrayedFrom parent, using polymorphic YAML deserialization (v2.1 []string, v2.3 []Strand) with isolated worktrees in /tmp/knossos-fray-* and 30-second creation timeout.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Entry point fraySession() in internal/cmd/session/fray.go executes 11-step pipeline (resolve parent, exclusive lock, load context, FSM validate, create child, write child, update parent with parking and strand append, emit events), with child wrap integration in wrap.go emitting strand_resolved and updating strand status to LANDED.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: ADR-0006 missing from disk, strand abandon resolution unimplemented, and nested fray (grandchild) behavior untested with multi-level fray trees.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Auto-parks parent to prevent concurrent mutation, implements fail-open worktree creation, children inherit state but not identity with strand tracking via ACTIVE->LANDED transitions, and event emission is non-fatal though ADR-0006 is referenced but missing from disk.

autom8y::knossos::feat/session-lifecycle.domain_summary: Session Lifecycle Management handles context continuity and coordinated state mutation in multi-agent CC workflows using a 4-state FSM (NONE→ACTIVE→PARKED→ARCHIVED), scan-based discovery, and a unified event system with schema versioning. The design trades WriteIfChanged behavior against file watcher triggers, supports dual read paths for event format compatibility, and implements non-transactional wrap operations across 10+ sequential steps without rollback capability.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Advisory flock unavailable on NFS/SMB, BufferedEventWriter is fire-and-forget, phase transitions forward-only, rotation preserves YAML frontmatter, snapshot never errors; key failures are lock timeout (ErrLockTimeout→ari session recover), FSM violations (ErrLifecycleViolation), BLACK sails quality gates at wrap (CodeQualityGateFailed with --force override), and multiple ACTIVE sessions (resolved via --session-id); mutation authority restricted to ari session CLI and Moirai agent.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Session state machine transitions through NONE→ACTIVE→PARKED→ARCHIVED (terminal) with orthogonal phase machine (requirements→design→implementation→validation→complete), complexity levels (PATCH/MODULE/SYSTEM/INITIATIVE/MIGRATION), execution modes derived from status and activeRite, and snapshot roles (orchestrator 10 entries, specialist 5+3) using v2.3 schema with procession and typed strands.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Core implementation spans `internal/session/` (22 files: FSM validation, context serialization, scan-based discovery, role-adaptive snapshots, 11 event types, tri-format reader) and `internal/cmd/session/` (41 files, 20 subcommands including create/park/resume/wrap/fray) with advisory flock(2) locks (10s timeout, 5-min stale threshold) and data flow managing validation→lock→FSM→save→emit→archive.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: TLA+ spec file location unconfirmed, ADR-0001/0022/0027 not located at docs/decisions/, ParkSource autopark hook value unverified, and procession lifecycle commands not traced.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Three ADRs (ADR-0001 FSM redesign with TLA+ spec, ADR-0022 scan-based discovery with lock format v2, ADR-0027 unified event system) establish session lifecycle design with explicit tradeoffs: WriteIfChanged prevents CC file watcher triggers (LB-001), dual read path for v1/v2/v3 event formats (LB-004), and scan-based O(n) discovery with no caching and non-transactional wrap.

autom8y::knossos::feat/tribute-generatio.domain_summary: Autom8y's Tribute Generation system converts ephemeral session data (context, events, sails, registry) into durable, machine-parseable summaries in dual formats: human-readable markdown with YAML frontmatter and JSON-serializable counts. The system derives summaries directly from facts in events.jsonl rather than agent impressions, implements idempotent generation with graceful degradation for missing sources, and ensures all operations produce valid output even with incomplete data. PURPOSE-AND-DESIGN-RATIONALE | Converts ephemeral session artifacts into durable machine-parseable summaries through fact-derivation from events.jsonl, supporting dual human/JSON consumers with idempotent overwrites and graceful degradation for missing sources. CONCEPTUAL-MODEL | Sources include required SESSION_CONTEXT.md and optional events.jsonl (tri-format), WHITE_SAILS.yaml, and artifact registry; event taxonomy maps tool.artifact_created/agent.decision/phase.transitioned/handoff/tool.call events to typed structures with duration calculated from endedAt minus startedAt and artifact types derived through event field cascade to metadata to path inference. IMPLEMENTATION-MAP | Four-file `internal/tribute/` package (types.go, extractor.go, generator.go, renderer.go) implements 11-step pipeline with CLI supporting three session resolution paths (--session-dir, --session-id, current directory) and YAML frontmatter plus 12 markdown sections. BOUNDARIES-AND-FAILURE-MODES | Hard failures on missing/non-directory session paths or unreadable SESSION_CONTEXT.md; graceful handling of missing events.jsonl, malformed JSON lines, missing WHITE_SAILS.yaml, and hardcoded schema "1.0"; known issues include last phase Duration=0, handoff collision resolution via last-wins, and --session-dir bypassing ProjectRoot for graduated artifacts. KNOWLEDGE-GAPS | Three areas not fully read: internal/artifact package internals, session.Context full schema, and renderer test coverage patterns.

autom8y::knossos::feat/validation-framew.domain_summary: Embedded binary-portable JSON Schema validation framework for structured artifacts with lazy-compiled schema caching and embedded filesystem routing. Two-tier validation architecture combines JSON Schema structural validation with YAML-configured handoff phase-transition criteria, supplemented by lightweight Go functions for performance-critical paths. purpose-and-design-rationale | embedLoader routes $ref resolution through embed:// URLs enabling portable schema validation; two-tier approach separates schema-structural (JSON Schema) from handoff-behavioral (YAML phase criteria) validation with Go fallback functions (ValidateSessionFields, ValidateSailsFields) for hot paths. conceptual-model | Lazy-compiling jsonschema.Compiler caches 11 schema files covering session-context, agent, complaint, prd, tdd, adr, test-plan, knossos-manifest, white-sails, handoff-procession, and handoff-criteria; ArtifactValidator performs type detection and frontmatter extraction while HandoffValidator evaluates per-phase per-artifact-type blocking and warning criteria. implementation-map | Six-file `internal/validation/` module implements Validator with embedLoader, artifact-specific methods (session/agent/complaint), ArtifactValidator (type detection + frontmatter extraction), HandoffValidator (3 evaluation modes), and SailsValidationResult; CLI subcommands in `internal/cmd/validate/` expose artifact, handoff, and schema validation. boundaries-and-failure-modes | Body content excluded from validation; no cross-artifact referential integrity checking; knossos-manifest.schema.json lacks Validator method; unknown phase-artifact combinations return Passed:true with zero checks; agent schema errors wrapped opaquely; ParseYAMLFrontmatter/ExtractFrontmatter duplication exists; ValidateBytes panics when compiler is nil. knowledge-gaps | ADR-0008 missing from disk; prd/tdd/adr/test-plan schema file contents unread; internal/cmd/handoff/prepare.go HandoffValidator usage pattern not examined.

autom8y::knossos::feat/white-sails-signa.domain_summary: White Sails Quality Gate Signaling is a proof-based session-end confidence system for preventing shipping unstable code, using three discrete colors (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK) determined by complexity-tiered thresholds across five proof types (tests, build, lint, adversarial, integration). The system reads physical log artifacts rather than agent self-assessment, applies downgrade-only modifiers, and integrates with CI/CD as a binary gate decision.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: System is read-only (no test/build/lint execution), Generator invoked at wrap time, ari sails check reads existing WHITE_SAILS.yaml without regeneration, Clew contract validation fails open, unknown complexity applies strictest thresholds, and Schema divergence exists (SCRIPT/PLATFORM in code but missing from enum).
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Two-phase coloring (computed_base + final) uses five proof types with PASS/SKIP/FAIL/UNKNOWN statuses, applying a blocking algorithm (blockers→BLACK, any FAIL→BLACK, UNKNOWN→GRAY ceiling, all pass→WHITE) then modifier and QA upgrades.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Six Go files in `internal/sails/` implement ComputeColor, complexity-tiered thresholds (7 levels), multi-framework proof parsing, Generator orchestration, CheckGate binary exit codes, and Clew contract validation; CLI via `internal/cmd/sails/check.go` with white-sails.schema.json schema.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Clew contract event type definitions incomplete, no ari sails generate CLI command identified, and session wrap lifecycle integration not fully traced.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Three-color proof-based signaling (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK) prevents Aegeus failures through complexity-tiered thresholds, with QA upgrade path (GRAY→WHITE with constraints and adversarial tests) and downgrade-only modifiers.

autom8y::knossos::feat/worktree-manageme.domain_summary: Git worktree management provides filesystem isolation for parallel development sessions using git-native detached HEADs, with ecosystem seeding on creation and shared knowledge symlinked from the main root. The system maintains dual metadata registries (central and per-worktree), integrates session context via worktree_id in SESSION_CONTEXT.md, and implements lifecycle operations (create, remove, cleanup) with age-based bulk removal and git pruning.

section_summaries.boundaries-and-failure-modes: Must invoke from main worktree (nested guard), enforces detached HEAD (no branch-per-worktree), Switch does not change shell directory, IsWorktree uses heuristic path-content detection, setupWorktreeEcosystem uses explicit os.RemoveAll before symlink, Pull is fast-forward only, all git operations enforce 30s timeout, no concurrent Create locking.
section_summaries.conceptual-model: Worktrees identified by type ID (wt-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-hex) with dual metadata registry (central .worktrees/metadata.json + per-worktree .knossos/.worktree-meta.json), self-healing via SyncMetadataFromFilesystem, session integration via worktree_id in SESSION_CONTEXT.md, and lifecycle spanning Create (git worktree add --detach + setupWorktreeEcosystem) through Remove and age-based Cleanup.
section_summaries.implementation-map: Six files in internal/worktree/ (worktree.go types, lifecycle.go Manager, operations.go setupWorktreeEcosystem, metadata.go dual registry, git.go 30s timeout, session_integration.go) with twelve subcommand files in internal/cmd/worktree/ (create, list, status, remove, cleanup, switch, clone, sync, export, import).
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: ADR-0010 and ADR-0029 referenced but not found on disk; internal/assets package missing from architecture inventory; session.IsValidComplexity not read in current codebase.
section_summaries.purpose-and-design-rationale: Eliminates file contention between parallel sessions via git-native isolation (detached HEAD, no branch proliferation) with ecosystem seeding on creation (full ari sync); shared knowledge (.knossos/, .know/) symlinked from main root while state directories (.sos/, .ledge/) created fresh per worktree.

autom8y::knossos::literature-agentic-kno.domain_summary: Organizational knowledge retrieval systems have evolved toward hybrid architectures combining sparse lexical matching, dense semantic embeddings, and graph-structured entity relationships, with agentic filesystem-based approaches remaining viable for well-structured codebases. This literature review synthesizes 2024-2025 research and production systems (GraphRAG, HybridRAG, RAPTOR, SCIP, ORD, MCP Registry) to identify architectural patterns, evidence-graded findings, and blind spots in the knossos platform's current filesystem-walking implementation. EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY | Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense embeddings via RRF) outperforms single-modality approaches with 87% vs 71-62% top-10 relevance; GraphRAG captures multi-hop entity relationships and community-level reasoning that flat vectors miss; agentic filesystem grep/find tools achieve >90% embedding performance on structured codebases but degrade in polyglot repos with inconsistent naming; knossos lacks semantic similarity, cross-repo entity resolution, and temporal decay—all addressable incrementally without wholesale replacement. SOURCE-CATALOG | Catalog documents five peer-reviewed papers (GraphRAG survey, RAPTOR hierarchical retrieval, HybridRAG dual-channel fusion), three production specifications (SCIP semantic indexing, ORD service discovery, MCP Registry federated metadata), and one Microsoft Research whitepaper on community-summary-based graph RAG, all with verification status and evidence grading for key claims. THEMATIC-SYNTHESIS | Five major themes emerge: (1) graph-structured knowledge representation explicitly captures entity relationships and domain hierarchies lost in flat embeddings; (2) hybrid dual-channel retrieval (vector + graph + sparse) requires fusion strategies like Reciprocal Rank Fusion to balance precision-recall tradeoffs; (3) hierarchical and multi-level retrieval (RAPTOR, GraphRAG community summaries) enables both detailed and synthesized answers across document granularities; (4) cross-repository entity resolution requires semantic versioning schemes (SCIP's package-version-symbol triples) rather than filesystem walking; (5) federated discovery protocols (ORD, MCP Registry) enable self-describing metadata catalogs but show ecosystem lock-in and limited generalization. EVIDENCE-GRADED-FINDINGS | Strong-evidence claims: entity graphs capture relational structure flat retrieval misses (corroborated across SRC-001, SRC-002, SRC-003, SRC-005); hierarchical tree-structured retrieval enables multi-level abstraction (ICLR peer review, 20% accuracy gain on QuALITY); cross-repository semantic navigation requires versioning-aware symbol identity (SCIP production system). Moderate-evidence claims: HybridRAG outperforms single approaches (single finance dataset); GraphRAG community-summary retrieval improves global sensemaking (Microsoft whitepaper, not peer-reviewed in full); agentic grep/find achieves >90% embedding performance (stated without production benchmarks); ORD and MCP Registry federate discovery (limited production adoption outside origin orgs). KNOWLEDGE-GAPS | Open controversies persist around: optimal knowledge freshness decay functions (no consensus published); cost-benefit of maintaining knowledge graphs at scale versus filesystem walking in polyglot environments; generalization potential of federated discovery protocols (ORD, MCP) beyond their origin ecosystems (SAP, Anthropic); evaluation benchmarks for organizational polyglot codebases (literature focuses on homogeneous document collections or single-language repositories); long

autom8y::knossos::literature-ai-coding-t.domain_summary: This literature review examines production AI coding tools and research systems that claim to "learn" from usage, distinguishing between actual model-level fine-tuning, context engineering (prompt injection), and marketing claims. The critical finding is that no production AI coding tool performs real-time per-user model fine-tuning; instead, "learning" occurs through aggregate model improvement from telemetry, persistent context files, or research-only self-modifying agent prototypes.

section_summaries.claude-code-anthropics-approach: Claude Code's learning occurs entirely through context engineering: hierarchical CLAUDE.md files (user-global, project-level, auto-generated MEMORY.md) and deterministic hooks that modify the prompt context across sessions, while model weights remain permanently unchanged and full transparency is maintained through readable configuration files.
section_summaries.comparative-analysis: Production systems unanimously rely on context engineering (persistent instruction files, semantic indexing, rule systems) rather than model-level learning; aggregate telemetry from millions of users influences periodic global model retraining; and per-user or per-project learning claims systematically refer to prompt injection mechanisms, not weight updates.
section_summaries.cursor-ai-code-editors: Cursor performs no per-user model fine-tuning and instead uses context injection via `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` files, semantic codebase indexing, and system prompt modification to create codebase-aware suggestions without modifying underlying model weights.
section_summaries.executive-summary: Production AI coding tools use three distinct learning mechanisms: aggregate model improvement from telemetry (GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q), context engineering via persistent instruction files (Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot custom instructions), and research-only self-modifying agents, with no real-time per-user fine-tuning occurring in any production system.
section_summaries.github-copilot: GitHub Copilot collects pseudonymous engagement telemetry (accept/reject signals, error logs, optional code snippets) that feeds into periodic global model retraining via synthetic data distillation and RLHF, while enterprise users have zero data retention, and the new agentic memory system provides repository-level persistent context verified against actual code locations.
section_summaries.key-findings-and-implications: The most effective "learning" in production is context engineering (curating what models see, not changing models themselves); acceptance-rate optimization can reduce suggestion quality through perverse incentives; and enterprise deployments consistently disable data collection, forcing vendors to rely on context-level personalization rather than model improvement pipelines.
section_summaries.other-notable-systems: Amazon Q Developer uses acceptance/rejection telemetry for global RLHF retraining and a Customizations feature for organization-specific fine-tuning on private repositories, while Windsurf, Replit Agent, and Zed optimize for context window efficiency and agentic architectures without per-user learning.
section_summaries.self-modifying-agent-systems: Research prototypes like Darwin Godel Machine and SICA demonstrate genuine self-improvement by rewriting their own code/prompts based on benchmark performance, but operate only in sandboxed research environments, not production coding workflows.
section_summaries.source-catalog: The review synthesized web research conducted 2026-03-11 across production systems (GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Amazon Q Developer, Windsurf, Replit Agent, Zed) and research prototypes (Darwin Godel Machine, SICA), with citations requiring independent verification for production use.

autom8y::knossos::literature-autonomous-.domain_summary: Autonomous agent dispatch patterns have emerged as a critical architectural domain in 2025-2026, with hardware-level isolation via Firecracker microVMs and gVisor becoming the standard for untrusted agent code execution, while Claude Agent SDK and OpenAI Codex represent the two leading programmatic dispatch paradigms with permission-tiered execution models. The literature converges on graph-based (LangGraph), role-based (CrewAI), and conversation-based (AutoGen) patterns for multi-agent orchestration, with "human-on-the-loop" governance using progressive trust escalation and budget envelopes replacing traditional human-in-the-loop approval workflows.

section_summaries.domain-calibration: Assess the maturity level, evidence quality, and pace of change in autonomous agent dispatch patterns.
section_summaries.evidence-graded-findings: Present key technical findings with explicit confidence levels (STRONG, MODERATE, WEAK) based on source corroboration.
section_summaries.executive-summary: Provide a concise summary of the domain's current state, consensus positions, and key architectural decisions.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Identify significant gaps where peer-reviewed evidence is sparse or practical guidance is underdeveloped.
section_summaries.methodology-note: Describe the review methodology, source selection criteria, and confidence assessment approach.
section_summaries.source-catalog: List verified primary sources with relevance ratings, verification status, and key technical claims extracted from each source.
section_summaries.thematic-synthesis: Summarize the major consensus themes across the reviewed literature sources.

autom8y::knossos::literature-causal-infe.domain_summary: Causal inference in spatiotemporal epidemiology addresses the challenge of identifying treatment effects when data exhibits spatial and temporal correlation, violating standard assumptions like SUTVA and parallel trends. The field has reached consensus that joint Bayesian disease mapping approaches outperform two-stage methods, though significant debate persists around spatial smoothing's role in causal identification and integration with machine learning remains nascent. source-catalog | Nine peer-reviewed sources (2018-2025) and one preprint establish the empirical foundation, with five papers rated highest relevance addressing spatial confounding adjustment, spatial interference via generalized propensity scores, universal difference-in-differences for non-continuous outcomes, Bayesian disease-mapping integration with DiD, and simultaneous treatment of unmeasured confounding with interference. thematic-synthesis | Five core themes emerge: (1) spatial confounding from unmeasured location-varying factors requires explicit adjustment beyond standard regression, (2) SUTVA violations via spillover/interference dominate spatial settings and demand bivariate exposure frameworks, (3) parallel trends assumptions fail for binary/count/polytomous outcomes common in epidemiology, (4) joint Bayesian modeling integrating disease mapping with causal frameworks outperforms sequential approaches, and (5) triangulation across multiple causal identification strategies provides the most robust evidence. evidence-graded-findings | Strong evidence supports that spatial confounding requires explicit adjustment, SUTVA violation is routine, and non-continuous outcomes invalidate parallel trends; moderate evidence backs joint modeling superiority, generalized propensity score sufficiency under interference, and time-invariant confounded identification in spatiotemporal processes. knowledge-gaps | Critical gaps include: causal machine learning integration with spatiotemporal data (affecting <1% of literature), optimal spatial treatment allocation strategies, formal guidance on interference structure simplification, confidence interval construction for synthetic control methods with spatial data, and empirical validation of Bayesian smoothing's causal identification consequences. domain-calibration | The literature demonstrates high internal consistency (95%+ agreement on core challenges), but external validity varies by outcome type and geographic scale, with small-area epidemiology showing stronger method fit than large-scale environmental applications due to inference complexity differences. methodology-note | This review synthesizes peer-reviewed literature with independent DOI verification and full-text confirmation; preprints included where they establish novel causal frameworks; machine learning approaches deliberately downweighted due to sparse spatiotemporal integration in published epidemiological literature. executive-summary | Causal inference in spatiotemporal epidemiology has evolved rapidly since mid-2010s with emerging consensus on three dominant challenges: spatial confounding from unmeasured location-varying factors, interference/spillover violating SUTVA, and parallel trends assumption inadequacy for non-continuous outcomes. Joint Bayesian models integrating disease mapping with causal frameworks show superior performance to two-stage approaches, and triangulation across multiple methods provides robust evidence, though active controversy persists regarding Bayesian spatial smoothing's causal identification role.

autom8y::knossos::literature-developer-c.domain_summary: Developer CLI distribution and dogfooding best practices encompass literature spanning community CLI design standards (clig.dev, 12 Factor CLI Apps), industry practitioner guides on internal developer tools, and peer-reviewed experience reports documenting structured dogfooding programs at scale. The domain establishes strong consensus that single-binary distribution (via Go or Rust) dramatically reduces adoption friction and that organized dogfooding—not ad hoc internal use—yields the highest-quality user feedback, though the intersection of these two practices remains sparsely covered in formal academic literature.

section_summaries.domain-calibration: Evidence quality is moderate overall with strong community consensus from practitioner guides (clig.dev, 12 Factor CLI Apps) and established CLI design authority, limited peer-reviewed work (two IEEE papers on dogfooding, one from 2006 and one experience report from 2019), and substantial technical documentation from GoReleaser and language-specific distribution tools.
section_summaries.evidence-graded-findings: High-confidence claims (STRONG/MODERATE): Go binaries at 2-5 MB with cross-compilation enable trivial distribution; single-binary distribution eliminates runtime dependency friction; dogfooding at scale uncovers unpredicted test scenarios; CLI design has rigorous principles (help, flags, versioning, configuration hierarchy); internal tools require product treatment with dedicated teams; micro-feedback loops (200x daily) offer highest optimization ROI; controversies remain unresolved: optimal language choice (Go vs. Rust vs. Node.js), appropriate analytics scope, and whether dogfooding alone constitutes sufficient validation.
section_summaries.executive-summary: Single-binary distribution via Go dramatically reduces adoption friction by eliminating runtime dependencies; structured dogfooding programs with 4,000+ participants uncover test scenarios missed by traditional QA and reveal geographic usage patterns; micro-feedback loops in developer tools offer highest ROI for optimization; CLI analytics with opt-out preserve developer trust.
section_summaries.knowledge-gaps: Literature lacks empirical comparison of Go vs. Rust vs. Node.js for CLI binary distribution at scale, formal studies of dogfooding's effectiveness relative to traditional QA, and guidance on balancing CLI analytics with developer privacy expectations.
section_summaries.methodology-note: This review synthesizes peer-reviewed papers, official community standards, authoritative practitioner blogs (Martin Fowler's site), official tool documentation, and technical tutorials; claims are graded by confidence level (STRONG/MODERATE/WEAK) and verification status, with recommendations for independent verification before production decisions.
section_summaries.source-catalog: Eight core sources identified: clig.dev (2020 community CLI standard), 12 Factor CLI Apps (practitioner guide by oclif creator), Guiding Principles for Developer Tools (internal platform tools at scale), Maximizing Developer Effectiveness (feedback-loop framework), IEEE ICGSE 2019 experience report (4,000-person dogfooding program), IEEE Software editorial on dogfooding history, Kartones on Go CLI advantages (2-5 MB binaries), and GoReleaser documentation (multi-platform release automation).
section_summaries.thematic-synthesis: Three dominant themes emerge: (1) single-binary distribution eliminates dependency friction and accelerates adoption, (2) structured dogfooding with geographic distribution and scale (4,000+ users) uncovers real-world scenarios and regional patterns that QA misses, (3) treating internal tools as products with platform teams and micro-feedback-loop optimization yields highest developer effectiveness ROI.

This domain synthesizes theoretical foundations with production engineering concerns including Byzantine fault tolerance, latency-consistency trade-offs (PACELC), and quorum flexibility in networked systems requiring ordered value agreement despite failures.

autom8y::knossos::literature-enterprise-.domain_summary: Enterprise AI-Powered Slack Integration synthesizes architectural patterns, security practices, and orchestration designs for building production AI agents within Slack's conversational interface. The domain covers HTTP-based event delivery, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines with permission-aware indexing, agent routing patterns, and emerging Slack AI platform APIs (2025) while identifying gaps in Go-specific implementations and feedback loop design. METHODOLOGY-NOTE | Literature review synthesizes official Slack documentation (5 sources), practitioner blogs and whitepapers (4 sources), and open-source libraries, with verification status noted per source but acknowledging LLM-synthesized attribution requiring independent verification before production use. EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY | HTTP Events API is recommended for production over Socket Mode due to horizontal scalability and Marketplace eligibility, while Bolt SDK serves as the canonical framework across Python/JS/Java with gaps for Go; Slack's 2025 agent-ready APIs (Real-Time Search, MCP Server, Work Objects) formalize thread-based AI surfaces, and enterprise RAG patterns combine permission-aware indexing with intent-based routing and multi-objective feedback loops, though Go integration patterns and production feedback design remain underdocumented. SOURCE-CATALOG | Nine authoritative sources (Slack official docs, SalesforceDevops analysis, slack-go library, Glean/Dust/Temporal engineering blogs) provide strong evidence for Slack platform mechanics and HTTP/rate-limiting guidance but weak evidence for emerging agent APIs and Go-specific production patterns. THEMATIC-SYNTHESIS | Production architectures prioritize HTTP-based event delivery with 1/sec message rate limits per channel, thread-scoped context via conversations.replies with thread_ts, and RAG pipelines enforcing permissions at indexing/retrieval time rather than post-generation; security requires LLM-to-LLM message blocking, OAuth least-privilege scoping, and AI disclaimers; orchestration uses Temporal for durable agent workflows with event-driven ingestion and mixture-of-experts routing to specialized agents. EVIDENCE-GRADED-FINDINGS | STRONG evidence supports HTTP over Socket Mode (10-connection limit, firewall constraints), 1/sec chat.postMessage limits, OAuth principle-of-least-privilege, RAG over fine-tuning for enterprise freshness/permissions, and permission-aware indexing; MODERATE evidence backs thread context via conversations.replies, reaction_added feedback collection, Temporal durable workflows, and hybrid keyword+neural retrieval; WEAK evidence characterizes Slack's Real-Time Search/MCP Server APIs, Dust's activity scale, and knowledge graph personalization benefits. KNOWLEDGE-GAPS | Production Go implementations lack Bolt SDK equivalents and pre-1.0 slack-go stability; feedback loop design (multi-objective optimization, reward modeling) is practitioner-documented but not peer-reviewed; Slack's 2025 agent-ready APIs remain in closed beta with limited public evaluation; mixture-of-experts routing effectiveness and permission-enforcement latency in large-scale deployments require field evidence. DOMAIN-CALIBRATION | This domain is maturing (Slack APIs announced late 2024, still evolving) with strongest evidence in platform mechanics (official docs) and moderate evidence in RAG/orchestration patterns (engineering blogs); Go-native and emerging agent API evidence is weak; production deployments at scale (Glean, Dust) provide architectural models but limited peer-reviewed validation of feedback loop design or Go SDK stability.

autom8y::knossos::literature-event-sourc.domain_summary: Event sourcing is an append-only architectural pattern for persisting application state as immutable domain events rather than mutable records, enabling strong auditability, write-scalability, and temporal queries at the cost of schema evolution complexity and eventual consistency challenges. The literature comprises two peer-reviewed empirical studies, authoritative vendor documentation (Microsoft, AWS), and influential practitioner works establishing event sourcing as appropriate only for high-throughput audit-critical systems, explicitly discouraged for simple CRUD domains. Evidence quality is moderate-to-strong, with identified gaps around GDPR compliance automation, long-term cost-benefit analysis, and polyglot event store comparisons.
